pr.title: Fix fresh-checkout `ninja` failure at the report step (#5)

summary: Fixed a fresh-checkout build break where the default ninja target (`progress`/`report` -> objdiff-cli report generate) crashed trying to open source-built base objects that don't exist when config.libs is empty (the all-asm byte-match baseline). One-line guard in configure.py disables the report target when no libs are declared, falling back to the sha256 `ok` check which correctly passes.
decomp_lessons: The curated objdiff.json (unit base_path list, used by the objdiff GUI and tools/gen_decomp_report.py) can reference source objects that config.libs never actually builds -- on a fresh checkout with no libs declared, `build/<ver>/base/` is empty and the ninja `report` target dies on the first missing object (e.g. crt/__init_cpp_exceptions.o).

main.dol building and byte-matching (`build.sha1: OK`) is independent from the ninja report step succeeding -- don't assume a green ninja run on a maintainer's machine (who tracks progress via tools/gen_decomp_report.py, not the ninja report) means the report target itself works for a fresh contributor.
smart_moves: Root-caused via a real repro (fresh checkout + ROM) rather than guessing, and cross-checked it against an independently reported Windows 11 failure of the same issue (issue #5) to confirm it wasn't macOS-specific.
Minimal, self-documenting fix: single `if not config.libs: config.progress = False` guard with an inline comment explaining why, instead of restructuring the build graph.

key_files.role: Added `if not config.libs: config.progress = False` so the ninja default target falls back to the sha `ok` check instead of the objdiff report generator when no source libs are declared yet.
